title: Dark Memory MCP — Main RFC (supersedes fragmented spec_id 141-145)

intent.what: Single canonical RFC for Dark Memory MCP. Replaces the five fragmented specs (141-145) that were persisted earlier as a flat CRUD-by-table design. This RFC defines intent-driven tools (~25), context-object retrieval, sequence-aware responses, six type-system invariants, dual-driver storage (SQLite default, Postgres opt-in), and a coexistent sibling relationship with dark-research-mcp.
intent.why: The earlier design was surface-level (CRUD per DB table). This RFC addresses the actual friction: the LLM has to call 5-7 tools in sequence to publish one artifact and gets flat rows back. New design: orchestrators as functions, context objects as return shape, sequence-aware `next` field, defense as type-system contract.
five_commitments: P1 Intent before CRUD — every tool answers an intent, not a DB op
P2 Context object — retrieval returns composed view, not row dump
P3 Sequence-aware — response includes `next` tool suggestion
P4 Defense as contract — six invariants at the Store interface boundary
P5 Economy by default — every retrieval runs Atlan dedup→filter→truncate→compress→cap

must_have: RFC published at vibe-flow/main/DARK_MEMORY_MCP_RFC.md and parseable
spec_id 141-145 superseded via dark_research_drift_log entries referencing this RFC
10 sub-specs created in dark.db referencing this RFC
Each sub-spec has its own spec_create + tasks + artifacts + drift check
dark-memory-mcp binary compiles + registers 25 tools + survives 1000-call stress
